legacySurfaceSanity.command: REPO="/Users/phaedrus/Development/cerberus-mono/cerberus"; if [ -f "$REPO/dispatch.sh" ]; then shellcheck "$REPO/dispatch.sh"; else echo "dispatch.sh retired"; fi && if [ -f "$REPO/bin/cerberus.js" ]; then node --check "$REPO/bin/cerberus.js"; else echo "node scaffolder retired"; fi

nonBlockingIssues.description: The feature still ships `Cerberus.TestSupport.LocalReviewRepo` from `cerberus-elixir/lib/cerberus/cli_test_support.ex`, so production builds package a git-mutating test helper that is only used by tests.

`resolved_snapshot/2` can still over-report `model.source` as `override` for fixed-model reviewers when only an unrelated pool tier override changed, so the redacted config evidence may overstate what was actually overridden.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/review-config.md` to document that `defaults/config.yml` still ships inert legacy top-level keys and to enumerate the loader's active merged config sections.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add explicit guidance for Elixir test-support modules: keep git-mutating fixtures and helpers in a test-only compile path instead of `lib/`, or document the rare exception when runtime packaging must include them.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `cli-ref-range-workspace` review found `cerberus-elixir/lib/cerberus/cli_test_support.ex` shipping a test-only fixture because no canonical test-support placement was documented or enforced.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Narrow the `cerberus-terminology` prerequisite so it applies to substantive terminology, prompt, or reviewer-vocabulary changes, not routine flag/help-text adjustments needed to satisfy the CLI contract.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `cli-ref-range-workspace` worker successfully completed a flag/help-text contract change without invoking `cerberus-terminology`, and the review concluded the current skill requirement is broader than necessary.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Remove or replace the required `refactoring-playbook` dependency because the skill is not available in this repository, forcing workers to deviate from procedure during structural refactors.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `review-core-cli-exit-contract` worker handoff reported `followedProcedure: false` because `.factory/skills/cerberus-cli-worker/SKILL.md` requires `refactoring-playbook`, but that skill is absent from the available repository skills.
